title: centaurea × borsodensis
intro: Centaurea × borsodensis, also known as Borsod buttonflower, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is a cross between Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea montana. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Centaurea × borsodensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Centaurea × borsodensis is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, to support overall health and well-being.
